roles.description: As the team grew, I stepped into a leadership role, setting technical direction and keeping our research and development moving forward, while staying hands-on with the work itself.
roles.title: Rendering Research Lead

roles.description: After finishing my Ph.D., I transitioned into the role full-time, continuing the rendering research and development work I had started during my studies.
roles.title: Rendering Research Engineer

roles.description: During my part-time role at Urus, I worked on rendering research and development with a focus on stylization, helping shape new techniques and tools. *
roles.title: Rendering Research Engineer

roles.description: Following the internship, I joined one of Google's research teams, collaborating on computer graphics projects and research papers. *
roles.title: Student Researcher

roles.description: I worked on real-time rendering and compositing tools and their integration into an existing platform. *
roles.location: Greater New York City Area, USA
roles.title: Engineering Intern

roles.description: Following my time at Snap, I joined a research team at CTU Prague, continuing to develop some of the ideas from that internship while also picking up new academic projects as I prepared to start my Ph.D.
roles.title: Researcher

roles.description: I joined Snapchat in Santa Monica, working on two real-time experimental projects for their product.*
roles.title: Research Intern

roles.description:
During my Master's studies I worked part time as a consultant, building custom software solutions for clients across various business processes.

My proudest project was an invoice automation system I built from scratch for Fortuna, the betting company. Starting from just a problem description, I developed an OCR-based solution that significantly reduced the manual workload of their accounting team.
roles.title: Consultant
